highlight.title: Cloudflare's Major BGP Outage Exposes Internet's Hidden Fragility
highlight.summary: In February 2026, a simple configuration change at Cloudflare accidentally withdrew customer routes across their global network, making websites unreachable for six hours despite healthy servers. This incident shows how the internet's routing system—the invisible infrastructure that directs your data—remains surprisingly fragile even at major tech companies.

resources.title: What Really Happens When You Type a URL in Your Browser? Step-by-Step Guide
resources.why_now: Perfect for beginners curious about the 'magic' behind everyday internet use. Shows the fascinating chain of events—from DNS lookups to TCP connections—that happens in milliseconds when you visit any website.

resources.title: How Networks Work: Exploring the Fundamentals of Switches, Routers, DNS, DHCP, NAT, VPN
resources.why_now: Comprehensive technical deep-dive covering the core protocols and hardware that make computer networks function. Essential for understanding how LANs connect to WANs and how data actually moves across the internet.

resources.title: BGP Handling Bug Causes Widespread Internet Routing Instability
resources.why_now: Real-world case study from May 2025 showing how a single corrupt BGP message triggered mass router shutdowns. Demonstrates the cascading failures that can affect global internet connectivity and why understanding routing protocols matters.

resources.title: Different Types of Networks: LAN, WAN, WLAN, VPN & VLAN Explained
resources.why_now: Updated for 2026 with Wi-Fi 7 and modern hybrid network architectures. Shows how businesses are combining wired LANs, wireless networks, and VPNs to create resilient, secure infrastructures in the remote-work era.

claude_take: The internet feels magical until it breaks—then you realize it's actually an incredibly complex system of agreements between thousands of networks, all trusting each other to route data correctly. Recent BGP outages remind us that despite decades of growth, the internet still relies on protocols designed when it was much smaller and more trusting.
